title: How to make Claude brutally honest
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 2 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftheanishajain_i-cant-believe-my-claude-is-finally-brutal-share-7462758762139832320-3Pjz%3Futm_source=tldrmarketing/1/0100019e4a385b73-29f367d0-97b0-4144-bb17-7dbd9a532988-000000/IgferuYoJ95BoLx3CX7tZ-0nYXdd97WgEFL8EuDrKLA=452
summary: How to make Claude brutally honest (2 minute read) This prompt turns Claude into a more rigorous, truth-first assistant that prioritizes accuracy over sounding confident. It avoids fabricating sources or numbers, flags unverified claims, and distinguishes facts from assumptions. It enforces transparency about what is known, unknown, or inferred, requires credible sourcing when possible, and pushes the model to avoid guessing, especially on recent or changing information.

title: Google's new Universal Cart wants to follow your entire shopping journey across the internet
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 2 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftechcrunch.com%2F2026%2F05%2F19%2Fgoogles-new-universal-cart-wants-to-follow-your-entire-shopping-journey-across-the-internet%2F%3Futm_source=tldrmarketing/1/0100019e4a385b73-29f367d0-97b0-4144-bb17-7dbd9a532988-000000/bQD7_LIK9hFjB0g1SEZ3ycG3BWgaXe8U1k6_Is9N2WQ=452
summary: Google's new Universal Cart wants to follow your entire shopping journey across the internet (2 minute read) Google's new Universal Cart lets users save products from across Search, Gemini, YouTube, and Gmail into a single hub that tracks price drops, flags compatibility issues, and alerts on restocks. The more significant announcement may be Agent Payments Protocol (AP2), which allows AI agents to autonomously complete purchases within user-defined spending limits and brand preferences.

title: Learnings from 100K Lines of Rust with AI
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fzfhuang99.github.io%2Frust%2Fclaude%2520code%2Fcodex%2Fcontracts%2Fspec-driven%2520development%2F2025%2F12%2F01%2Frust-with-ai.html%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/zgYwec80lhODeEKpCSwztOheHxrRznM5fvHghRzBbnE=452
summary: Learnings from 100K Lines of Rust with AI (9 minute read) Using AI coding agents, a new Rust-based multi-Paxos consensus engine was developed with over 130,000 lines of code, modernizing the Replicated State Library. This spec-driven approach achieved a massive performance boost, increasing system throughput from 23,000 to 300,000 operations per second while ensuring high reliability through automated testing.

title: The React2Shell Story
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 10 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flachlan.nz%2Fblog%2Fthe-react2shell-story%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/usV9pPn-lwJf1fuiomXfltbeZKfmGoYSP69eH6LfyHo=452
summary: The React2Shell Story (10 minute read) A security researcher describes finding React2Shell, a critical remote code execution flaw in React's undocumented “Flight” protocol used by Server Components and Next.js. Over a week, he and a collaborator discovered that the protocol's handling of complex objects and JavaScript “thenables” could be abused to hijack React's internals and chain function calls into arbitrary code execution. He reported it to Meta on November 30, and Meta confirmed it with...

title: Everything in C is undefined behavior
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 13 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fblog.habets.se%2F2026%2F05%2FEverything-in-C-is-undefined-behavior.html%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/_q6W0hHTMGLLbTE-FeO3egtUBocsK9Nx-WSRcmT36xc=452
summary: Everything in C is undefined behavior (13 minute read) The prevalence of undefined behavior in C/C++ makes writing truly correct code nearly impossible, leading to systemic vulnerabilities across platforms. To mitigate these risks, developers should use LLMs to audit codebases for subtle flaws that escape human detection.

title: How to Write "Garbage Code"
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 7 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fread.engineerscodex.com%2Fp%2Fhow-to-not-write-garbage-code-by%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/giIOfG94u5PVEiG63j0-1Cy-3rXLtY_tDE0NRjphn94=452
summary: How to Write "Garbage Code" (by Linus Torvalds) (7 minute read) Linus Torvalds, the creator of Git and Linux, says that overly complex or abstracted code, like unnecessary helper functions, makes code maintainability and understanding worse. It increases cognitive burden and, in some cases, it makes sense to repeat code to reduce human and LLM context switching. Code should be optimized for readability and ease of iteration.

title: The Tacit Dimension: Why Your Best Engineers Can't Tell You What They Know
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 12 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcekrem.github.io%2Fposts%2Fthe-tacit-dimension%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/jd4vELe0ma4Bo45gQzzFGj3eosUEzQe1AdW6xJvWUtc=452
summary: The Tacit Dimension: Why Your Best Engineers Can't Tell You What They Know (12 minute read) Software engineering expertise relies on tacit knowledge, an intuitive understanding that AI cannot grasp since it is only trained on explicit text and code. Over-reliance on AI risks losing such important architectural knowledge, making senior engineers and apprenticeship irreplaceable for transmitting these complex, unwritten insights.

title: Stop worrying about how to configure agents. Focus on what they deliver
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: Sponsor, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgo.prose.md%2Ftldr-dev%3Futm_source=tldr-dev%26utm_medium=newsletter%26utm_campaign=openprose-2026-launch%26utm_content=secondary-w1%26utm_term=headline/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/aq1DiXfPaBJMCEyBqoRySE6qMFbJvMecq7gGK2leMgU=452
summary: Stop worrying about how to configure agents. Focus on what they deliver (Sponsor) Move the agent conversation from how (prompts, MCP servers, harness configs) to what with the open-source language for AI sessions: OpenProse. All you do is declare outcomes in plain English and agents self-configure to pursue it. Try it in your terminal: npx skills add openprose/prose. See the project →

title: Qwen3.7: The Agent Frontier
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 16 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fqwen.ai%2Fblog%3Fid=qwen3.7%26utm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/uGEMwj9dWMRFp_pvPdeYN0BTB4WgChpyR9RzpdNWBkA=452
summary: Qwen3.7: The Agent Frontier (16 minute read) Qwen3.7-Max is a foundation model designed for autonomous AI agents, excelling in high-level reasoning and complex automation tasks like software engineering and office productivity. Its main feature is prioritizing long-horizon execution, allowing it to sustain coherent strategies across thousands of tool calls and multi-hour optimization tasks.

title: Distributed Systems Testing Skills
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fshenli%2Fdistributed-system-testing%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/ghOGPQnA2JkSWWhaEZXdhiq2RY2BFknrt0xbJBfYJC8=452
summary: Distributed Systems Testing Skills (GitHub Repo) This repository introduces two specialized skills for AI coding agents to automate the planning and execution of tests for distributed and stateful systems. The methodology prioritizes claim-driven testing, where specific product promises are validated against complex failure modes like network partitions, concurrency issues, and crash-recovery scenarios.

title: Incident Report: May 19, 2026- GCP Account Suspension
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fblog.railway.com%2Fp%2Fincident-report-may-19-2026-gcp-account-outage%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/Cg_SSiNJvQ4cnfz9KGPOw0_fT4xeIFT9dr4KVxig75s=452
summary: Incident Report: May 19, 2026- GCP Account Suspension (9 minute read) A total, eight-hour service outage for Railway on May 19 was caused by Google Cloud incorrectly suspending its production account, which cascaded across all regions as network route caches expired. Restoration required a staged recovery of infrastructure, and future plans include adopting a true mesh network and distributing core dependencies across multiple vendors.

title: An OpenAI model has disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 11 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FxN03XH/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/bi7fHKZ0TRm2t74atxbwDeg8WgApcxbwgK8D-uztdxw=452
summary: An OpenAI model has disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry (11 minute read) An OpenAI reasoning model has disproved the planar unit distance problem, a central conjecture in discrete geometry first proposed by Paul Erdős in 1946. This breakthrough marks the first time an autonomous system has solved a prominent open mathematical problem.

title: Structural Backpressure Beats Smarter Agents
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-21, Reading time: 12 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Freubenbrooks.dev%2Fblog%2Fstructural-backpressure-beats-smarter-agents%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e4a4a0475-3accf979-d86a-48bd-8836-710c2952d4e0-000000/CYrpPziYlNOBGAQTx2B-qXknh0GK0qKZXSHGj6v-B78=452
summary: Structural Backpressure Beats Smarter Agents (12 minute read) Shen-Backpressure integrates security invariants into system architecture using a formal specification language to enforce rules via deterministic "guard types." This structural approach forces AI agents to iterate until code satisfies underlying logic.
